data.title: Fed is still reviewing Morgan Stanley request for lower stress capital buffer
data.description:
While the Federal Reserve released its list of final capital requirements for large banks, effective Oct. 1, 2025, it's still reviewing Morgan Stanley's (NYSE:MS) request for a downward adjustment in its stress capital buffer, the Wall Street bank said on Friday.

The final calculations were determined by the central bank's annual stress tests, the results of which were released in June. The stress capital buffer is the amount of capital that the largest U.S. banks are required to set aside to weather a potential economic downturn.

"Morgan Stanley (NYSE:MS) remains actively engaged with the Federal Reserve to reach a final SCB requirement before the October 1, 2025 effective date," the company said in a statement.

Morgan Stanley (MS) stock ticked down 0.1% in Friday after-hours trading.

data.title: Canopy Growth establishes $200 million at-the-market program
data.description:
• Canopy Growth (NASDAQ:CGC) has established a new at-the-market equity program that allows it to issue and sell up to US$200 million of common shares of the company from treasury from time to time in concurrent public offerings in the United States and Canada.
  •  As per the terms of the program sales of common shares in the ATM program in Canada is limited to aggregate gross sales proceeds of up to $50 million and in no event will the combined gross sales proceeds of the ATM Program in the United States and Canada exceed $200 million.
  • CGC -11.11% after hours to $1.6.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Fed declares individual capital requirements for large banks, DB USA tops list
data.description:
The Federal Reserve Board announced on Friday the final individual capital requirements for large banks, effective October 1, with Deutsche Bank (NYSE:DB) USA topping the list.

The new requirements come in the wake of the Fed's stress test results.

Minimum capital requirement is the same for each bank, and stands at 4.5%.

Deutsche Bank (NYSE:DB) USA logged the largest stress capital buffer requirement in the list. The requirement is based in part on the stress test results and is at least 2.5% for each of the banks.

Notable common equity tier 1 capital requirements are listed below:

  • Deutsche Bank (NYSE:DB) USA - 16.0%
  • Citigroup (C) - 11.6% 
  • JPMorgan Chase (JPM) - 11.5%
  • Goldman Sachs (GS) - 10.9%
  • Bank of America (BAC) - 10.0%
  • UBS (UBS) - 9.7%
  • HSBC (HSBC) North American Holdings - 9.6%

In April, the Fed proposed to average major banks' stress test results over two consecutive years to reduce the Y/Y changes in capital requirements.

If the board finalizes the rule as proposed, this year's stress test results will be averaged with those from 2024, and updated capital requirements will be published separately.

data.title: DIH Holding US receives Nasdaq deficiency notice for delayed filings
data.description:
• DIH Holding US (NASDAQ:DHAI) said on Friday that it received a Nasdaq notice on August 26 for failing to file its Form 10-Q for June 30, 2025, and its delayed Form 10-K for March 31, 2025, breaching Listing Rule 5250(c)(1).
  • The company has until Sept. 29, 2025, to file the delinquent reports or submit a compliance plan. If accepted, Nasdaq may grant an extension until January 12, 2026.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Monopar Therapeutics files $300M mixed securities shelf
data.description:
• Monopar Therapeutics (NASDAQ:MNPR) filed $300 million mixed securities shelf.
  • Filing

data.title: Everbright Digital receives Nasdaq deficiency letter
data.description:
• Everbright Digital (NASDAQ:EDHL) confirmed on Friday that it received a Nasdaq notification, stating the company that it is no longer in compliance with Nasdaq's minimum bid price requirement rule of $1.00 per share for continued listing.
  • The notification letter has no immediate effect on the company's listing on the Nasdaq Capital Market at this time.
  • The company has been granted a compliance period of 180 calendar days, or until February 23, 2026, to regain compliance.
  • The company's business operations are not affected by the receipt of the Notification Letter. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Hyperscale Data announces "at-the-market" offering of common stock
data.description:
• Hyperscale Data (NYSE:GPUS) has established an "at-the-market" equity offering program under which it may sell, from time to time, shares of its common stock for aggregate gross proceeds of up to $125 million.
  • The company intends to use a majority of the net proceeds from this offering, if any, to acquire Bitcoin and to further develop its Michigan data facility.
  • The company also intends to use a smaller amount of the proceeds to acquire XRP, the digital asset developed by Ripple Labs and native to the XRP Ledger
  • GPUS -12.55% after hours to $0.466.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: GreenPower receives determination letter from Nasdaq
data.description:
• GreenPower Motor (NASDAQ:GP) announced on Friday that the company received a Nasdaq determination letter stating that the company has not regained compliance with Nasdaq Listing Rules.
  • The Letter stated that, unless the company requests an appeal of this determination on September 3, 2025, the staff has determined that the company's common shares will be scheduled for delisting from Nasdaq and will be suspended at the opening of business on September 5, 2025, and a Form 25-NSE will be filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission which will remove the company's common shares from listing and registration on Nasdaq.
  • The company intends to request a hearing before the Panel to appeal the Letter. A hearing request will stay the suspension of the company's common shares and the filing of the Form 25-NSE pending the Panel's decision.
  • GP -1.47% after hours to $0.335.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: WNS shareholders approve Capgemini acquisition with overwhelming majority
data.description:
• WNS (Holdings) (NYSE:WNS) said its shareholders overwhelmingly approved the proposed acquisition by Capgemini SE (EUR: CAP) at both the Court Meeting and General Meeting held on August 29, 2025.
  • Approximately 99.9% of shares voted were in favor, representing ~79.2% of WNS' total outstanding shares as of the record date.
  • The $76.50 per share all-cash transaction, announced on July 7, 2025, values WNS at $3.3 billion, excluding net financial debt.
  • The deal is expected to close before year-end, subject to customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals.
  • “We are pleased with the results of today’s Court Meeting and General Meeting and thank our shareholders for their strong support of this transformative combination,” WNS CEO, Murugesh said in a statement.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Nasdaq slides 1% as chip stocks weigh, with Wall Street posting a marginal weekly loss
data.description:
Wall Street on Friday fell, weighed down by a slide in semiconductor stocks. Market participants also received a key in-line inflation reading that did little to change expectations of a Federal Reserve interest rate cut in September.

The negative session contributed to overall slight weekly losses for all three major averages. 

The benchmark S&P 500 gauge (SP500) fell -0.6%, while the blue-chip Dow (DJI) slipped -0.2%. 

The Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) slid -1.2%. Marvell Technology (MRVL) slumped -18.6% and ended as the top percentage loser on the tech-heavy index after the chipmaker's quarterly report disappointed investors. Other chip stocks also came under pressure. 

Meanwhile, Nvidia (NVDA), the world's largest chipmaker, declined -3.3%. A report said Chinese tech giant Alibaba (BABA) had developed a new artificial intelligence (AI) chip to help fill in the absence left by Nvidia (NVDA) in the Asian market.

Also weighing on markets was Dell Technologies (DELL). The stock was -8.9% amid concerns over the PC and electronic maker's quarterly AI server margins.

“Given all the blood pressure medication taken in anticipation of Nvidia’s earnings this week, it was almost written in the stars that the week should close more or less flat for each of the major indices. The market now heads into a seasonally weak month; that said, since April the only surprises have been to the upside, so trading the calendar seems unwise; price will tell us where price is going," Alex King, investing group leader of Cestrian Capital Research, told Seeking Alpha.   

A busy economic calendar was the focus on Friday, with the spotlight on the July core personal consumption expenditures (PCE) price index. Widely regarded as the Fed's preferred inflation gauge, the core PCE deflator rose +0.3 M/M, flat from June and matching the consensus estimate. On a Y/Y basis, it rose +2.9%, also in line with consensus but ticking up from June's +2.8% reading.   

The largely tame inflationary signal keeps the Fed on track for an interest rate cut in September, according to the CME FedWatch tool. 

Speaking of the Fed, an emergency court hearing over Fed Governor Lisa Cook's lawsuit challenging President Trump’s attempt to remove her from the board ended with no ruling on Friday.  

Turning to the fixed-income markets, U.S. Treasury yields were mixed. Longer-term maturities gained, while shorter-term instruments slipped, keeping the yield curve steep. The benchmark 10-year yield (US10Y) was last up 3 basis points to 4.23%, while the more rate-sensitive 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 1 basis point to 3.63%.

For more, see how Treasury yields have done across the curve on the Seeking Alpha bond page.
